definitions.text: The foremost side of something or the end that faces the direction it normally moves.
The side of a building with the main entrance.
Located at or near the front.
examples: The front runner was thirty meters ahead of her nearest competitor.

definitions.text: Pronounced with the highest part of the body of the tongue toward the front of the mouth, near the hard palate (most often describing a vowel).
examples: The English word dress has a front vowel in most dialects.

definitions.text: To face (on, to); to be pointed in a given direction.
To face up to, to meet head-on, to confront.
Used to summon a worker on duty, such as a bellhop.
